relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus returns to Capernaum and the house becomes known.
The crowd fills the space while he speaks the word.
Four carry a paralyzed man toward him.
Blocked by the crowd, they open the roof and lower need into the room.
Jesus sees their trust and speaks release before movement.
The scribes reason in their hearts.
They hear release as blasphemy.
Jesus knows the hidden reasoning and names it.
Release and rising are held together.
The Son of Humanity shows authority on earth to release.
The man is told to rise, carry the mat, and go home.
He rises immediately, and the room turns to wonder.
Jesus returns to the sea and teaches the crowd.
Levi is called from the tax office into following.
The table opens to tax collectors, misaligned ones, disciples, and Jesus.
The boundary-keepers question the table.
Jesus names himself physician for those who know their need.
Fasting becomes the next question.
The bridegroom’s presence changes the time.
When the bridegroom is taken, fasting will return.
New cloth cannot simply patch the old.
New wine needs fresh skins.
Hungry disciples pluck grain on Sabbath.
The Pharisees read the moment as unlawful.
Jesus remembers David’s hunger.
Sacred bread once served need beyond ordinary boundary.
Sabbath is gift for the human, not the human a slave to Sabbath.
The Son of Humanity is lord even of Sabbath.
